generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, constants) visible in the AADL model or defined within the current AGREE annex.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
When defining ranges, use `int`, `real`, or `bool` types for constants and helper variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq` to define helper variables for complex expressions.
Ensure all identifiers are correctly typed (int, real, bool).
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL elements as part of a repair.
Avoid adding package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to place guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
